title: Red Hat Security Advisory: mariadb:10.3 security update
overview:
An update for the mariadb:10.3 module is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.2 Advanced Update Support.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

affected_products: Red Hat Enterprise Linux AppStream AUS (v. 8.2)
full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: mariadb:10.3 security update

An update for the mariadb:10.3 module is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.2 Advanced Update Support.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

An update for the mariadb:10.3 module is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.2 Advanced Update Support.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

Updated Packages:
cfa4f87b.src.rpm
c7510d9c.src.rpm
9df5005f.src.rpm
cfa4f87b.x86_64.rpm
c7510d9c.x86_64.rpm
9df5005f.x86_64.rpm

CVE-2025-13699: A flaw was found in MariaDB. This vulnerability allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code on affected installations via improper validation of a user-supplied path prior to using it in file operations in the mariadb-dump utility, requiring user interaction.
